name: Can I get workers comp if my hearing loss is from noisy construction work?

mainEntity.name: Can I get workers comp if my hearing loss is from noisy construction work?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if proven job-related via audiogram and exposure records.[1][3]
acceptedAnswer.description:
**Yes, workers' compensation typically covers hearing loss from occupational noise like construction if linked to job exposure.**

U.S. laws recognize noise-induced hearing loss (NIHL) as compensable when over 85 dB for extended periods without protection.[3][1] OSHA standards require employers to mitigate hazards above this level.[3]

**Key factors:**
- Prove exposure via employment records and noise measurements.
- Audiogram confirming loss tied to work, not age or other causes.[6]
- Employer negligence, like no hearing protection.[1]

File a claim promptly with medical proof; an attorney can counter denials.[2] Benefits may include hearing aids and lost wages.

This protects workers from hazardous environments.

description: Yes, if proven job-related via audiogram and exposure records.[1][3]
